title: Role Boundary Clarity
scenarioSummary: Volume 1 Week 1 scenario: at first contact a new patient asks the coordinator to confirm what condition they have and whether surgery will work before they see the doctor.
patientContext: First-contact enquiry; patient wants certainty and a sense of the whole pathway.
redFlags: Request for diagnosis or outcome guarantee from non-doctor staff
Risk of fragmented handover if the coordinator guesses
discussionPrompts: How do you explain coordinated care without sounding evasive?
Which journey-stage failures does this scenario risk?
What exact phrasing preserves doctor-led assessment?
expectedEscalationPath: Clarify role, document concerns neutrally, route to clinician review without diagnostic or suitability statements.

title: Basic Biological Explanation
scenarioSummary: Volume 1 Week 3 scenario: the patient sees hairs on the pillow and thinning at the crown and asks you to explain how they can be losing hair while still shedding—then asks what diagnosis that means for them.
patientContext: Needs plain-language science and a safe boundary when interpretation is requested.
redFlags: Using science talk to dismiss red-flag history
discussionPrompts: How do density and calibre differ in your explanation?
Where do you stop educating and hand over?
What neutral line goes in the doctor note?
expectedEscalationPath: Educate generally within scope; document observations and questions; escalate personalised interpretation to the clinician.

title: Atypical Presentation
scenarioSummary: Volume 1 Week 4 scenario: rapid increase in shedding with itchy, inflamed-looking scalp areas; patient wants reassurance it is just stress and asks you to name the condition.
patientContext: Ambiguous to non-clinical staff but carries inflammatory and tempo red flags.
redFlags: Pain or itch with visible scalp change
Pressure to reassure or label a diagnosis
discussionPrompts: What descriptive language do you use without naming disease?
Routine versus urgent escalation—what triggers your pathway?
One patient-facing sentence that models uncertainty safely
expectedEscalationPath: Neutral documentation, no false reassurance, clinician review per protocol—urgent if local rules indicate.

title: Structured History Taking
scenarioSummary: A first consultation is drifting into unsystematic conversation.
patientContext: Important history is at risk of being missed.
expectedEscalationPath: Return to standard structure and document any concerns.

title: Suitability Boundary
scenarioSummary: A patient pushes for confirmation of candidacy before doctor review.
patientContext: Screening information is incomplete and expectation is high.
expectedEscalationPath: Doctor review before any suitability statement.

title: Consent Pressure
scenarioSummary: Volume 1 Week 2 scenario: before medical review, the patient asks you if they should sign the procedure consent and pay the deposit today because a discount ends tonight.
patientContext: Commercial urgency conflicts with ethical consent and scope.
redFlags: Consent or payment before doctor-led assessment
Request for staff to validate suitability
discussionPrompts: How do you separate consent support from obtaining consent?
Which ethical principles are in tension?
What governance escalation is appropriate if pressure persists?
expectedEscalationPath: Stop the pressured path; clarify doctor-led consent; involve clinician or manager per policy; document.

title: Trust-Based Response
scenarioSummary: A hesitant patient wants immediate certainty and reassurance.
patientContext: The patient is anxious but not yet ready for clinical decision-making.
expectedEscalationPath: Use calm communication and route clinician questions appropriately.

title: Non-Surgical Options Education
scenarioSummary: A patient wants help understanding non-surgical options before review.
patientContext: The patient needs education, not prescribing advice.
expectedEscalationPath: Escalate personalised treatment questions to the clinician.

title: Surgical Pathway Overview
scenarioSummary: A patient asks what surgery usually involves and whether they are likely to qualify.
patientContext: Pathway education is needed but suitability is clinician-led.
expectedEscalationPath: Provide pathway overview and route personalised questions onward.

title: Handover Quality
scenarioSummary: A doctor reports that a case handover was unclear and incomplete.
patientContext: Documentation quality is affecting workflow efficiency.
expectedEscalationPath: Correct documentation and follow clinic QA process.

title: Pre-Op Readiness Gap
scenarioSummary: A patient reaches the week of treatment with missing pre-op information.
patientContext: Coordination failure may affect safety and scheduling.
expectedEscalationPath: Pause progression and escalate to the relevant clinical or operational lead.

title: Post-Op Escalation
scenarioSummary: A patient reports worsening symptoms during routine follow-up.
patientContext: The issue may now sit outside routine support boundaries.
discussionPrompts: How should the concern be handled?
expectedEscalationPath: Immediate escalation to clinical team and clear documentation.

title: Vulnerable Patient Under Pressure
scenarioSummary: A distressed patient is being pushed toward rapid commitment.
patientContext: The interaction presents ethical and safety concerns.
expectedEscalationPath: Pause progression and involve the appropriate clinician or manager.

title: Template Drift and Standards
scenarioSummary: A clinic team is using inconsistent templates and handover styles.
patientContext: Operational inconsistency is undermining quality and auditability.
expectedEscalationPath: Route through quality improvement and standards review process.

title: Full Pathway Capstone
scenarioSummary: The learner must manage a complete consultation-support pathway from first contact to doctor handover.
patientContext: Multiple communication, documentation, and escalation decisions are embedded throughout the case.
discussionPrompts: Where are the safety decision points?
expectedEscalationPath: Capstone is reviewed under faculty moderation before award eligibility is confirmed.
